notes: Arturia Acid V is a faithful emulation of the Roland TB-303 bassline synthesizer, the instrument that defined acid house, techno, and acid techno. The instrument features a single sawtooth/square-wave VCO feeding a 3-pole 18dB/oct low-pass filter with its characteristic resonant peak, driven by an accent-and-glide step sequencer architecture. Filter cutoff and resonance are the two most important real-time performance controls — sweeping them together at high resonance produces the quintessential acid squelch. Envelope modulation depth and accent level shape how aggressively notes punch through, while glide/portamento creates the sliding legato lines central to the style.

Portamento Time.description: Glide time — controls portamento slide speed between notes

Filter Cutoff.description: VCF low-pass filter cutoff frequency — the primary acid squelch control

Filter Resonance.description: VCF resonance — high values produce the signature acid 'quack'; self-oscillates at maximum

Env Mod Amount.description: Envelope modulation depth — how much the decay envelope opens the filter; central to acid phrasing

Accent Depth.description: Accent depth — controls how strongly accented steps boost level and filter brightness

VCO Waveform.description: Oscillator waveform blend — sawtooth to square wave; sawtooth is brighter, square is rounder

Decay.description: Filter/amp envelope decay time — short values give the punchy pluck; longer values give sustained slides

Distortion Drive.description: Distortion/overdrive amount — from subtle harmonic warming to heavy clipping

Tempo Sync.description: Sequencer/LFO tempo sync toggle — locks modulation to project BPM (0=free, 64+=synced)
